page: Amazon Web Services m5zn.2xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8252C processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs (4 physical cores with hyperthreading) operating at a high clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The CPU includes 24 MB of L3 cache and supports AVX-512 vector instructions. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data shows average performance across most standard compute and memory operations. The single-core stress-ng score is 1869.99, and the multi-core score is 9854.71. Single-threaded gzip compression is a notable strength, reaching 31.88 GB/sec, which places it in the top 25% of tested instances. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is relatively weak at 36.06 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth tests show 549.11 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 54.44 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Large-model LLM inference is a significant bottleneck, with 7B model text generation performing weakly at 7.44 tokens/second.

Network connectivity is established with a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m5zn.2xlarge offers a high-frequency CPU profile within a balanced 1:4 memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it suitable for workloads requiring high single-thread performance without the premium of memory-optimized configurations. The absence of local storage means all storage operations rely on network-attached EBS volumes. This instance is configured for workloads such as web serving, database operations, and applications requiring high clock speeds and moderate memory capacity.
description: Amazon Web Services m5zn.2xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8252C processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs running at a high clock speed of 4.5 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance has no local storage or GPU. Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities across most workloads, with a single-core Geekbench score of 1565.0 and a multi-core score of 5869.0. It exhibits strong single-threaded gzip compression at 31.88 GB/sec, though multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and medium-sized LLM inference are weaker. With a 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, this instance is suited for workloads requiring high clock speeds and balanced memory, such as web serving, database operations, and general-purpose applications relying on network-attached storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5zn.2xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring an 8-vCPU Intel Xeon processor at 4.5 GHz, 32 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for balanced workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5zn.2xlarge is an 8-vCPU, 4.5 GHz Intel Xeon general-purpose instance with 32 GB RAM and 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-frequency general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores and balanced memory for compute-intensive and network-optimized workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8252C processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs running at 4.5 GHz
32.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for EBS connectivity
Strong single-threaded compression performance of 31.88 GB/sec
No local storage or GPU, relying entirely on network-attached storage
Suited for web serving, database operations, and high-frequency compute tasks
